item.title: Trump reverses course on 20% fee for Strait of Hormuz cargo
item.description: Trump said Tuesday he "decided to replace" a 20% fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, which he announced a day before, with trade and investment deals from Gulf states.

item.title: New York enacts nation's first statewide moratorium on building new data centers
item.description: Building new data centers in New York will be paused for a year in order to allow state officials to establish guidelines protecting residents and the environment, Gov. Kathy Hochul said.

item.title: ICE halts most vehicle stops following deadly shootings, sources say
item.description: Neither of the victims of the ICE shootings in Maine or Texas were the target of enforcement operations, according to the Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: Police say husband asked spellcasters to hex his now missing wife
item.description: Shortly before Maya "May" Millete vanished, authorities say her husband Larry messaged a spellcaster, "Please punish May and incapacitate her enough so she can't leave the house."

item.title: Where's Maya Millete? Family, friends continue search for missing California mom
item.description: A California mother disappears without a trace – did her husband try to have a hex put on her so she wouldn't leave him?

item.title: Who could replace Lindsey Graham in the Senate after his sudden death?
item.description: South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham's sudden death late Saturday has set off a scramble for who will succeed him in the Senate.

item.title: Ex-firm partner says "people would be blown away" by real story of "The Wolf of Wall Street"
item.description: "The Real Wolf of Wall Street," a new documentary from See It Now Studios on Paramount+, focuses on the real-life story of infamous stockbroker Jordan Belfort and his brokerage firm Stratton Oakmont. Howie Gelfand, a former partner at Stratton Oakmont, explains why he decided to be part of the documentary and how the 2013 film, "The Wolf of Wall Street," starring Leonardo DiCaprio, got the real story wrong.

item.title: DoD watchdog finds 155mm artillery plant built 2 years ago has produced nothing
item.description: An ammunition plant in Mesquite, Texas, has not produced any metal projectile parts after the Army spent $469 million to establish the facility.

item.title: Alan Ritchson gives inside look at the film "Motor City," which has nearly no dialogue
item.description: Alan Ritchson talks about starring in "Motor City," which has nearly no dialogue, how his process was different and why he thinks it will resonate with audiences.

item.title: Planning your summer vacation or staycation? Here's how to save.
item.description: With the cost of gas and airfare higher than last year, Kelly O'Grady has money-saving tips to help you plan your dream summer vacation or staycation.

item.title: New data shows inflation cooled more than expected in June
item.description: Recently released Labor Department data indicates inflation cooled more than expected in June due to falling gas prices following U.S. diplomatic efforts with Iran. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: Supreme Court Justice Elena Kagan recalls interactions with Lindsey Graham during nomination process
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Elena Kagan took a moment before her opening remarks on Capitol Hill to honor the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, recalling her nomination process and her interactions with the lawmaker.

item.title: Watch: Supreme Court Justice Elena Kagan's makes case for more security funding
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett are on Capitol Hill to testify before House lawmakers in response to a request for increased security funding amid growing threats against members of the highest court.

item.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
item.description: Investigators say her Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.

item.title: Can creditors empty a joint checking account over unpaid debt?
item.description: Sharing a bank account could mean sharing exposure to a partner's unpaid debts. How does that work, though?

item.title: United's newest economy upgrade guarantees an empty middle seat
item.description: United Airlines has a solution to passengers squabbling over who gets the armrest: empty middle seats.

item.title: Warsh vows to tackle inflation in first testimony as Fed chairman
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh told the House Financial Services Committee that the central bank has "no tolerance for persistently elevated inflation."

item.title: E. Jean Carroll receives $5M sex abuse, defamation payment from Trump
item.description: A jury concluded in 2023 that Trump should pay Carroll $5 million in damages.

item.title: Shippers balk at Trump's Strait of Hormuz fees: "Dangerous Pandora's Box"
item.description: Industry insiders say "there is no legal basis" for Trump to impose a 20% fee on cargo transiting the Strait of Hormuz, which could cost tens of millions of dollars per ship.

item.title: Folarin Balogun addresses FIFA decision to lift red card suspension
item.description: U.S. soccer star Folarin Balogun told "CBS Mornings" he "was in shock" when he received a red card in a World Cup match and discussed FIFA's decision to lift the one-game ban.

item.title: Justices Kagan and Barrett ask Congress for more Supreme Court security funding
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett are asking lawmakers to raise the court's security budget following an increase in threats. Jan Crawford has more details on their rare testimony.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Scammers are using FaceTime to pose as banks and steal your money. Here's what to watch for.
item.description: Consumer advocates say they're tracking a rise in cases where scammers are using FaceTime. The scams often start with a text about suspicious activity on your bank account or credit card. Ash-har Quraishi explains.

item.title: Maps show extreme heat and flood threats across U.S.
item.description: Much of the U.S. is facing either extreme heat or excessive rainfall and potential flooding on Tuesday. Here's where the greatest threats are expected.

item.title: Trump says Reflecting Pool has been drained for repairs
item.description: President Trump said the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was drained for repairs, after weeks of railing against alleged vandals.

item.title: Military action against Iran formally restarted last week, Trump told lawmakers
item.description: President Trump formally notified Congress that "military action" against Iran restarted last week in a letter obtained by CBS News, as a monthslong ceasefire comes to an end.

item.title: Darline Graham, Lindsey Graham's sister, appointed to fill Senate seat
item.description: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster announced his pick to fill the vacancy left by Sen. Lindsey Graham's death.

item.title: Dr. Céline Gounder: Lindsey Graham died of aorta disease. My husband did too.
item.description: Lindsey Graham's aorta tore at 71. Grant Wahl's burst at 49. One is common and age-driven; the other is inherited, silent, and findable.

item.title: Georgia homeowners are being forced from their homes to help power AI data centers
item.description: Some homeowners in Georgia say they have been given an ultimatum: sell your home or the state will take it. Utility giant Georgia Power is planning to build a new transmission line so it can handle more data centers. Skyler Henry reports.

item.title: | Searching for Maya Millete
item.description: A young mother disappears. Investigators say her husband contacted spellcasters to put a hex on his wife so she wouldn't leave him. "48 Hours" contributor Jonathan Vigliotti reports. | Case update: On July 9, 2026, a jury found Larry Millete guilty of first-degree murder in the death of Maya Millete.
